question: Will CPI rise more than 0.4% in April 2026?
reasoning: Multiple sources indicate significant inflationary pressures building for March 2026 with energy prices spiking due to Middle East conflict. Recent data shows CPI increased 0.3% in February after 0.2% in January, establishing an upward trend. Energy prices rebounded 0.5% in February after decline in January, and war-related oil price surge (Brent +63% in March) will likely drive April CPI higher. Food inflation is also accelerating with USDA raising 2026 forecast to 3.6%.
key_factors: Recent monthly CPI trend showing acceleration: 0.2% Jan, 0.3% Feb
Energy price surge from Middle East conflict - oil up 63% in March
Food inflation pressures increasing - USDA raised 2026 forecast to 3.6%
Shelter inflation persistent at 3% annually, major CPI component
March CPI forecast at 3.1-3.2% YoY vs 2.4% in February
risks: Energy prices could moderate if conflict resolves quickly
Seasonal adjustments could dampen reported monthly increase
Core CPI remains relatively stable suggesting limited spillover effects
Base effects from prior year comparisons
Federal Reserve policy response could impact expectations
